normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_subtree_witness (t : Tree) (p q a : Int)
  (hb : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
  (hl : lcaBst t p q = some a) :
  ∃ sub : Tree,
    IsSubtree sub t ∧
    (match sub with
     | Tree.nil => False
     | Tree.node _ k _ => k = a) ∧
    HasKey sub p ∧
    HasKey sub q
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When the BST contains both keys, the returned key roots a subtree of t that still contains both queries.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_lowestness (t : Tree) (p q a : Int)
  (hb : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
  (hl : lcaBst t p q = some a) :
  ∀ d : Tree,
    IsProperSubtree d t →
    (match d with
     | Tree.nil => True
     | Tree.node _ k _ => k = a → False) →
    HasKey d p →
    HasKey d q →
    False
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No proper subtree strictly below the returned root can still contain both query keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_equal_keys (t : Tree) (p : Int)
  (hb : IsBst t) (hp : HasKey t p) :
  lcaBst t p p = some p
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When p equals q, the LCA is that shared key.
